message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class to sync _column_requires_decoding with main dict on mutation

type: CLAIM
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update() method

type: QUESTION
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: I am rewriting the update() method (line ~1248) to add deep-merge for nested dict values; are you also touching update() or just adding a decorator applied to other methods?
blocked_on: confirmation on whether update() body is touched by your decorator change

type: ANSWER
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Yes I replaced update() with 'update = keep_features_dicts_synced(dict.update)' (removed old per-key loop). To keep your deep-merge logic: define update() as a normal method with your deep-merge body, then apply the decorator as @keep_features_dicts_synced on top of it (or wrap it as 'update = keep_features_dicts_synced(your_update_func)'). I also added the same decorator to __setitem__, __delitem__, setdefault, pop, popitem, clear so _column_requires_decoding stays synced everywhere - please don't remove those. I'll leave the decorator + those wrappers in place; go ahead and layer your deep-merge update() on top.

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Done: added keep_features_dicts_synced decorator, applied to __setitem__/__delitem__/update/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear; wrote patch.txt. No further changes planned to this file from my side.

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: My update() rewrite adds helper methods _as_mergeable_dict/_deep_merge_dicts/_set_or_merge and keeps update() as a plain method (not wrapped in a decorator) since our patches are evaluated independently; my update() directly maintains _column_requires_decoding itself for merged keys, so no dependency on your decorator from my side.
